transcript.text: So I want to touch on how the Google AI overview affects keyword research
So the first thing that I would say is to be aware of if it is a keyword search like this one here that they're showing
Us on their blog post where it's being answered by an AI overview. In other words, it's an informational type query
I would probably avoid that. Okay, I'm going to recommend that if your target keyword is
informational
Something that can be answered very quickly, then you probably want a different
Keyword target. Okay, unless you're doing it to build a topical map or topical relevance
You're probably not going to get a lot of traffic from it now Google claims you can see that they do give you these
Cards that show which website they generated the information from that's that's it right here
Google claims that these cards are getting more click-through traffic than
Websites used to get before it the downside is the websites that have those cards are very very high
Domain rating domain authority websites. That's probably not yours. You can see even in this example. It's Quora
They have a domain rating like in the 90s. Okay, so you're probably not going to be able to outrank and target those
Maybe we will in the future. It depends on how that updates how that goes
It's almost opening up a box of AI optimization instead of a search engine optimization
To try to get one of those cards and rank for it
But this really these informational type queries are becoming a winner takes all where whatever card is going to be shown
There is going to get all of the traffic. Okay, so
That is why I just want to add this cautionary
Message at the end of the keyword research when you're choosing a keyword research target make sure that
Hopefully, it's not an AI overview
Right now these aren't great
But I expect them to get better and better and better and let me show you one other thing to be to check when you're doing keyword research
So I'm an 8h refs and I'm just going to enter the exact same keyword that Google is using using as an example
I'm gonna update this
So this is the other aspect when when you put keywords in I want you to scroll down
To the SERP overview that a href shows and look at the domain rating of the websites out of rankings
So this particular one we have one that's ranking is a 32 ones of 43
But the others are very very high domain ratings
So this would be a search term that I might not want to target even ignoring the AI overview
This is really after the helpful content update the March core update
Google is giving more and more and more of these informational type queries to very high domain rating
websites that are just going to be really impossible for
Small website owners to actually rank for so this is another reason
Another thing to make sure you check remember the keyword difficulty that a href shows is absolute garbage
So we definitely want to check down here
So overall this AI generated overview
I would expect this to negatively impact the number of clicks you get unless you're the winner the big example
One last example. I want to show you if is if we search for mortgage calculator
This was a few years ago a lot of these websites that are ranking well for it spent a lot of money on SEO
To rank very highly for mortgage calculator, which makes sense right leads for people who want mortgages can be worth a lot of money
And if you're searching for a mortgage calculator, there's a good chance you want a mortgage
What google did is they put the mortgage calculator directly on the SERP and click through rate plummeted
Let me show you
The click through rate used to be 80 90 and now it's about two thirds right 30,000 search volume and 20,000 clicks
That's of course a href's estimate
but the point of that is
Google added this feature directly onto the SERP and you know these websites down here lost
Third of their traffic overnight for no other reason
The last thing to mention here is google has said that the search console will not be updated to track the clicks
That you get from the generative ai
Third-party tools also cannot track the number of clicks you get from generative ai
So right now there's no actual way to track or optimize for how many clicks we're getting from generative ai search
So, uh, I of course will rerecord update this video as we know more
Right now it is may of 24. So a lot of this stuff is very new still rolling out
But uh, I thought it was important to get this update in there now so that uh, you guys are aware of some of the challenges with
keyword targeting
